description: Sprints 7, 8, and 9 ran the full /trine-eval:harness-sprint loop end-to-end without invoking the council fan-out documented in `.council/config.json` as `review_frequency: every-sprint`. Sprint 7 has a sprint-prebrief file; Sprint 8 and Sprint 9 do not. No post-sprint course-corrections file exists in `.council/course-corrections/` for any of Sprints 7-9. The Phase 1.5 plan insertion (DEC pending) was authored by the orchestrator in a /trine-eval:harness-sprint context, not via a council position paper + nemawashi. This is a Method-axis drift: the stated cadence is per-sprint, observed cadence is per-three-sprints (and only when the user manually invokes /henkaten-council:council-autorun). The risk is corrective lag: scope-guardian and architect findings about sprints 7-9 have not been recorded, and any drift that occurred has accumulated unaudited across three sprints.

evidence.claim: review_frequency in .council/config.json is set to every-sprint.
Only sprint-07.json exists in .council/sprint-prebrief/; sprint-08.json and sprint-09.json are absent.
decision-log.jsonl contains 7 DEC entries, all from sprint-context 0, 6, or 7; no entries for sprint-context 8 or sprint-context 9.
Sprints 7, 8, 9 all completed PASS at 100% weighted, so the drift was not surfaced by a sprint failure that would otherwise force council attention.

The Phase 1.5 plan amendment (Sprint 9 insertion, sprints 9-11 renumbered to 10-12) was decided unilaterally by the orchestrator within a /trine-eval:harness-sprint context, not via a council position paper. Reversibility is reversible (git revert of 497f184), but the change is structurally similar to DEC-0002 (spec amendment) and DEC-0004 (sprint reordering) which both went through nemawashi-equivalent ratification.
